results.question: How does the Tsallis-Pareto distribution handle high-pT tails compared to standard Boltzmann-Juttner?
results.answer_preview:
> "The Tsallis non-extensive distribution function accounts for the power-law contribution at high-pT and this could be understood as empirically taking of QCD contributions." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Section II.A [[1]]()

> "The use of Tsallis non-extensive statistics incorporates a power-law distribution, which has a dominant contribution at higher collision energies, making a better descript
results.answer:
> "The Tsallis non-extensive distribution function accounts for the power-law contribution at high-pT and this could be understood as empirically taking of QCD contributions." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Section II.A [[1]]()

> "The use of Tsallis non-extensive statistics incorporates a power-law distribution, which has a dominant contribution at higher collision energies, making a better description of the transverse momentum spectra of charged particles and identified particles up to very high values of pT." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Section I [[1]]()

> "It is found that the Tsallis distribution generally leads to a better description for the complete transverse momentum spectra whereas the BGBW model explains the bulk part of the system." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Abstract [[1]]()

> "The BGBW model is quite good in explaining the bulk part of the system, however it fails at low-pT which could possibly be due to the decays of hadronic resonances." — Rath_2020_1908.04208.pdf, Section I [[1]]() (Note: While the text mentions failure at low-pT due to resonances, other sections clarify it fails at *high* pT where the power law dominates, as seen in the summary: "Conclusively, the BGBW explains the bulk part of the transverse momentum spectra... whereas the non-extensive Tsallis statistics describes the charged particle transverse momentum for the complete pT-range") [[2]]()

> "The identified particle spectra at RHIC and LHC do not follow Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution due to the possible QCD contributions at high-pT." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Section I [[3]]()

> "To describe the complete transverse spectra of identified particles, one has to account for the power-law contribution at high-pT and this empirically takes care for the possible QCD contributions." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Section I [[3]]()

> "In the limit of q → 1, Tsallis distribution (Eq. 3) reduces to the standard Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution (Eq. 1)." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Section I [[3]]()

> "The resultant expression [Hagedorn/Tsallis-like] acts as an exponential and a power-law function for small and large pT, respectively." — Khuntia_2019_1808.02383.pdf, Section I [[3]]()
